name: Maned Wolf
primary_sources: International Union for Conservation of Nature
Brazilian Institute of Environment and Renewable Natural Resources

visual_attributes.morphology: Tall, slender canine with long legs and a distinctive mane
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Weighs 20–30 kg, up to 110 cm at the withers
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Long legs adapted for tall grasslands, large ears, and a mane on the nape

domain_specific_visual.FOOTPRINT_SHAPE: Triangular plantar pads with small digit marks
domain_specific_visual.SKULL_FEATURES: Reduced carnassials, long canine teeth, prominent sagittal crest

functional_attributes.primary_function: Seed dispersal of fruits, especially the wolf apple

relationships.description: The bush dog is the closest living relative of the maned wolf.
The maned wolf is native to South American regions.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Known for its unique appearance and role in seed dispersal
primary_sources: International Union for Conservation of Nature
